title: 1a. Foreword
content: Dear Reader and appreciator of the Fine Art of Cataloguing, I thank you for taking the time to peruse this tome. I intend for this to be a reference for all who should wish to visit the world known as Irithiel, though depending on the manner of your travel, arrival and the specific timing involved, you may find the information contained herein to be confusing, overwhelming, or common knowledge; nonetheless I commit this to words specifically because I know that there are those who will find great utility in what I have compiled. Although I have seen many ages of this world, and have amassed the knowledge of a multitude of creatures, I (your humble scribe and editor) am but one being; there will be entries in this repository that are incomplete, and others that are missing- alluded to, but not yet written. Rest assured that this is an ongoing project, and will be brought to completeness before existence fades away in the end. Regardless of the opinions you may develop about my art, please know that I treasure every moment that your eyes gaze upon my work. Spending even a second of your insignificant lifespan here fills me with great energy. In reading more than three words of the above or of any given article, you have already agreed to our exchange. I won't take more than I need to, as I hope to do business with you again in the future; each donation of your life allows me to add more articles, after all. So do come back. Reyvan the Knowlegeable.

title: PI
content: Chronological term in The Shining Vale: The period after the Conquest of the Vale in VR 492 by Callamus the Righteous.

title: VR
content: Vale Reckoning (VR) is the calendar system used in the Shining Vale during the time of the Revannian Monarchy. VR 1 began at the coronation of King Aeonath the Civilizer, leader of the heroes who pacified the Vale, in the settlement of Haut-Revanne. VR 1 corresponds to AY After the Conquest of the Vale VR stopped being monitored, with the entire period being referred to as PI (Post-Invasion)

title: Conquest of the Vale
content: The Conquest of the Vale in VR 492 resulted in the destruction of Duchy Revanne, the erasure of the history of the dwarves in The Shining Vale, the sealing of the cities and abandonment of the towns in Duchy Redhill and widescale warfare in Duchy Corwyn. Duchy Truscia had been warned ahead of time in an event called Il Patto Del Diavolo, or the Devil's Bargain, and so they surrendered immediately and were completely spared. The Conquest was lead by Callamus the Righteous, leading an army of Celestials named The Golden Fist. The Fist were left in charge after Callamus departed, with the Celestials leaving, instead drawing recruits from the mortal citizens. The Conquest, historically framed as a liberation from Monarchy, resulted in the erasure of the religion of St. Aeonath.

title: The Jade City
content: Some 3000 miles Northeast of Corwyn City, Duchy Corwyn, The Shining Vale. Capital of a Yuan-Ti civilization. Notes: Near the Northeast corner of the continent.

title: Bassarian Common Tongue
content: As Bassaria was the first kingdom of mortals, they did attempt to formalise a consistent language. Not spoken widely, Bassarian common was used mostly for court and priestly records as written accounts were a valuable commodity. Many of the surviving texts are located in The Aethenium as they were collected by, and often written by, Apkallu Who Reads, who became Aethenius, the Scholar. After the Sundering of the Lands, Bassarian common was still used in by the functionaries in the city of Bassaria, but eventually declined after the city fell.

title: Cimmerian Common Tongue
content: The Cimmerian Empire used a specific language for their official state communications, though local areas may have used a separate language for day-to-day duties, particularly if they were not a predominantly human culture. This language is still the common tongue for any of the former colonies or inheritor countries after the Fall of Cimmeria, including those listed below. Sendria Hellenburg The Shining Vale Port Rain It is a minority language in the following countries, as either it was never the official language, or if cultural tongues are taken over. It will still likely be understood by some fraction of the people. Lausanne Killiburry Uxtatli

title: Isthariel the Channeler
content: As a note to future civilizations. If you forcibly oust a magically powerful leader, make sure- I mean absolutely sure- that you kill them. Otherwise that's how you get carnage gods. Do you want carnage gods? -Reyvan Isthariel was a powerful Potentate in the Cimmerian Empire, using her magical prowess to dominate a large section of the city and suppress any potential challengers for almost 200 years. During this time, she managed to defeat her only equal, Quidun, by using guile to waylay him long enough to create an Obelisk that channeled large amounts of power to herself (by tapping what is now the active volcano at Uxtatli), allowing her great resilience, regenerative power, and magical energy. Isthariel was eventually exiled by a cabal of lesser wizards and divine practitioners, forcing her to flee from her own palace onto the plane of Limbo, where she spent the timeless days preparing; it is there that she encountered agents of an Elder God, Orryx, who told her the story of the rise of Thalassus and the fall of the ancient culture of Bassaria. This information taught Isthariel that there was a way to gain even greater power and get revenge on her foes. In pursuing this goal over the sleepless timescape of the plane, the merciless Isthariel lost any remaining vestige of kindness or restraint under the sanity-draining tutelage of Orryx’s acolytes. Shortly after this she ascended fully and returned to the world as the God Zatar. Her wrath was the cause of the fall of the Cimmerian Empire, as she systematically destroyed all those who opposed her, and anyone around them. Her final act during her Ascent to Godhood was to cast the City of Cimmeria down the ground from the heights, burying it into the landscape and creating a large bay North of what is now Port Rain, and vertically approximately above Applewood. Several lesser gods, such as Jaraxx, the Rot, attempted to intervene, so once Cimmeria was in ruins, Zatar turned her eyes to the gods as the new source of her unbridled, raging, insanity.

title: The Luxan Empire
content: The Luxan Empire existed in the distant past in The Eastern Continent, having fallen long before the elves of The Woad arrived. Their sites are clearly constructed on intersections of lines of power. From the archeological evidence, they appear to have been mostly Tabaxi, with with a sizable slave-caste of dwarves.

title: The Tale of Hazidkal
content: This excerpt was taken from the ramblings of a group of Githyanki studying a manifested castle on a floating island, in the middle of Limbo. To clarify for the uneducated, this was a group of insane space pirates sitting in anarchic enemy territory where reality doesn't exist unless you will it to, staring at paintings, food, windows, statues, and such- all of which sprang only from the mind of their leader- in order to try to access a device supposedly of Hazidkal's making. I think a grain of salt, as it were, is suitable. -Reyvan The Archon Hazidkal originally lived in the Republic of Joyous Openings, on the Fair Shore of the Land of Unspeakable Glory. After being elevated to the highest position in the Admiralty of the Republic, Hazidkal made several massive technological leaps that aided the Republic in its wars against the Ungodly Combine. The final of these was the means of traveling backwards through the strands of fate by unweaving them back to a focal node, and recreating them; altering the events of history by living them in a different way than was originally destined to happen. But such power is addictive- using this freely to try to fix the numerous problems that inevitably appeared, one after another, farther and father backward on the weave of fate, Hazidkal found herself impossibly far in the past; Guradji, a place with significantly less advancement and thus subject to dominion. It was here that she created the first Obelisk. Hazidkal, empowered by her Obelisk, eventually went far enough back, and grew in power to the point that she ascended and became Lachesis, thus creating the weave that she herself had unraveled and re-traversed so many times.

title: Piety - Optional Rule
content:
Piety is an Optional Rule from the Mythic Odysseys of Theros sourcebook for 5th Edition Dungeons and Dragons, published in 2020.
Some of the Deities presented in this text are adapted from Deities found in that book, but made more suitable to the world of Irithiel; but most of the Deities have been created herein, drawing of course on the mythology of cultures found in the original and most popular campaign setting, Terra.

title: That Which Has Consumed Its Own Name
content: It is exciting to think that one of the oldest ideas, the oldest priorities, the oldest important things- is a secret. It makes one wonder. -Reyvan This Entity is an Elder God Regional Names: None Domains: Not Available For Divine Powers. Regional Worship: None Nature: We know this being exists by the vacuum left by its presence, and by the occasional being, mortal or otherwise, who becomes touched by it. They come to know things they shouldn't, and in the end, go mad. We, like those touched beings, ultimately know next to nothing about it. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: None Relations to other Deities: Enigmatic at best. Origins: Do I need to complete every section? title: The Earth Mother Irithiel
content: When I first stepped foot upon her, she was still hot from her Ascension. Painful, even for one like me. She's really developed since then, but I miss the days when an errant mortal would speak her name in vain, and the ground would open up and swallow him whole. Good times. -Reyvan The Earth mother is an Elder God Regional Names: Gaia (Thracia), Terra (Thracia), The Mother (Dwarven Cultural), The Red (The Dal), The Green (Druidic Cultural), Green Mother (Elven Cultural), Mama Yondalla (Halfling Cultural) and many more. Domains: Life, Nature, Trickery, War. Regional Worship: The Earth Mother is worshipped almost universally, and so has very little dedicated clergy. Nature: The original god, the Earth Mother is the Goddess of the Land, the Wild and the Hunt. She is serene, stable, and giving to those who maintain the balance of nature against civilization; ruthless, mercurial and uncaring to those who knowingly violate or exploit it. It is hard to describe her activity in the world, as she is literally the world. What is known is that she is gracious, and suffers many wounds on behalf of mortal and God-kind alike, with no retribution. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Ahksha, the Western Wind, Aspect of Existence Douma, the Southern Wind, Aspect of Erosion Faria, the Northern Wind, Aspect of Upheaval Estes, the Eastern Wind, Aspect of Renewal Chiriki, the Huntress, Aspect of the Wild Damu, the Green, Aspect of Fertility Relations to other Deities: Origins: The Earth Mother predates all known history and her early history can not be divined magically. It is likely that it can not be known, or can only be known by Lachesis or one of the Winds. Activity: All. What does one say? Desired Behaviour in Followers: Tending to life, participating in the circle of life in the way of the wild, reining in civilization, and stamping out undeath are some of the very few things known to please the Earth Mother. The most common druidic lifestyles seek to emulate this, for which they are clearly rewarded. Piety Benefits:

title: Tiamat
content: Tiamat is an Elder God

title: Astere of the Night Sky
content: Astere's behaviour reminds me of the Fey; extreme in all dimensions, mercurial and alien (even to me). But still predictable, in a way. -Reyvan Astere is a Major God Regional Names: Selene, The Moonmaiden, She Who Sees What You Hide, Lady Dark Domains: Twilight,Trickery,War Regional Worship: Nightwatchers (Thracia), Sisters of the Moon (The Shining Vale) Nature: Astere, Goddess of the Night Sky, sees everything that happens in the darkness when Helios leaves the world. She is sometimes, a protector of the weak, sometimes a keeper of secrets, sometimes the hider of secrets, but always a lover of mystery. Astere, like most Major Gods, does not interact directly with the world for fear of damaging it. But she is very active through her agents, her aspect deities, and those of other Major Gods friendly towards her and her goals. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Frouros, the Watchman, Aspect of Vigilance Paratiritis, the Seeing, Aspect of Observation Trikar, the Shrike, Aspect of the Assassin Relations to other Deities: Astere is assumed to be the counterpart of Helios, but the two do not have an obvious relationship. Lady Dark, ever mysterious, is difficult to shed light on. Origins: Try as I might, Astere's origins are somewhat mysterious, and there is no written record nor cultural memory of her origin or any disaster her Ascent to Godhood may have caused. She was first noticed after the Sundering of the Lands in ancient Bassaria; prior to that event the sky had always been the domain of Helios- with so much more territory to cover, he could no longer illuminate everyone and when he first moved away and the sky grew dark, Astere could clearly be seen. It could be that she had always been there, or that she moved to fill the void of Helios' absence. Activity: Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits: You increase your piety score to Astere when you expand the goddess’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these: Keeping a dangerous secret despite personal cost Revealing a critical truth at an important moment Mediating a major dispute or conflict Aiding the weak or impeding the strong Your piety score to Astere decreases if you diminish Astere’s influence in the world, upset the balance of power, or make her look biased or petty through acts such as these: Revealing a secret that should remain unknown Selfishly refusing to share information Showing favoritism toward any other god or group Furthering the aims of another god in defiance of Astere’s will -Astere’s Devotee Piety 3+ Astere trait. As a devotee of Astere, you have proven yourself a devotee of reality’s mysteries and laws. You can cast detect magic with this trait a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), or with a spell slot. You regain all expended free uses when you finish a long rest. In addition, you know the mage hand cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.  -Astere’s Votary Piety 10+ Astere trait. You can cast detect thoughts once with this trait, or with spell slots, requiring no material components. You regain your expended free use when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for this spell. In addition, you have advantage on saving throws against being charmed.  -Astere’s Disciple Piety 25+ Astere trait. As a true champion of Astere, you are aware of everything that goes on around you. You gain 30' blindsight. -Champion of the Night Piety 50+ Astere trait. You can increase your Constitution or Intelligence score by 2 and increase your maximum for that score by 2.

title: Chorai the Stillborn Dead
content: I'm very excited by this new prospect; given a few hundred years to properly form, she might just shake things up around here. -Reyvan Chorai is (probably) a Major God Regional Names: None Domains: Arcana, Death, Grave, Order, Peace Regional Worship: None. Nature: Chorai is the nascent goddess of Death, Undeath and the Undying. Prevented from emerging in full, this new goddess is in a state of torpor; only time will tell if they grow enough to accept any true position in the pantheon. As of latest reckoning, Chorai has not yet interfaced with mortals, so no understanding of her exists at large. Her existence is known, however, to a few learned individuals. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Pan, The Soulgatherer, Aspect of Death The Legion of the Dead, Aspect of Undying Relations to other Deities: Origins: Having experimented with necromantic magic for decades, and having learned from Quidun the Wise and Benificent in his demi-lich state following the Fall of Cimmeria, the Sendrian wizard Soza Hakade attempted to use the Obelisks of Power in the way that Quidun had originally planned, which was to Ascend. Altering the Obelisks to draw their power from afar, she amassed magical power while also amassing political power as the head of the Magesterium Panopticon in the country of Sendria. Her plans were rushed, and eventually thwarted, by a group of adventurers known as The Seekers. Enough of the process had occurred, however, that her Aspect Deities had already emerged. Their continued presence in the world is the largest piece of evidence that Chorai still has a role to play. Given the unexpected ascension of the God Rrakash at this time, it has been theorized that the divine energy was divided between them, slowing the ascension of both and giving them time to withdraw before calamity could occur. Activity: None Desired Behaviour in Followers: None Piety Benefits: Unknown

title: Helios the Radiant Truth
content: Helios is a boring god, with boring ideals and a boring fascination with mortals; but there are very few more powerful than he. Perhaps it is good that he's so preoccupied. -Reyvan Helios is a Major God Regional Names: Lux (The Luxan Empire) Domains: Death, Life, Light, Order, War Regional Worship: Lawbringers (Thracia). Most current mortals worship his Aspects rather than Helios directly. Nature: The Luxan's eponymous God of the Sun, Helios is the paragon of truth, justice, and beauty. He believes in progress, the improvement of mortal lives and the progression of their collective skills. Helios and his coterie, the Muses, are directly involved in the arts, medicine, and the ingenuity of invention. Helios has a deep and unflagging love of mortals, to the point that I suspect he created them to begin with. For this reason, he is one of the few Major Gods active in the mortal realm (through his muses and other agents, and his own distant presence, of course). Helios often interferes to change things as he thinks they should be changed; or enlists mortal heroes to do the same, whether they are willing or not. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Aesclepius, the Mender, Muse of Healing Aesthenes, the Glorious, Muse of Beauty Parvati, the Accepting, Muse of Love Themis, the Righteous, Muse of Justice Relations to other Deities: Origins: The second oldest of the Major Gods, it's known from archaeological evidence that Helios was venerated by The Luxan Empire, meaning he predates the first major civilization. Given his remit, it is likely that he gifted civilization to mortals, and The Luxan Empire was the result. Helios, unlike the Earth Mother and the other Elder Gods, is known to have Ascended, which is why he is classified as a Major God; the Winds have made this fact clear, but refuse to discuss it further. Due to the passage of time and the Sundering of the Lands, there is no record of the catastrophe caused by his Ascent to Godhood, but the lines of power that run below the ancient Luxan sites may be evidence of what happened given their veneration of the Sun God. It is generally assumed that Helios is, in all real meanings of the word, the Sun. Activity: Helios above almost all else loves Recently, Helios is suspected to be involved in the creation of The Dry Lands and The Wastes of Subdurni, both in The Eastern Continent in or near The Shining Vale. His motives for this activity are not known, but adherents of him and his muses are common in the area, and a child of his, Callamus the Righteous, conquered The Shining Vale in DR 570, leaving an occupied puppet state in place. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Helios rewards ambition, the pursuit of knowledge, the pursuit of justice and the appreciation for the good things in life. Piety Benefits: You increase your piety score to Helios when you expand the god’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these: Carrying out punishment on a fugitive from justice Exacting vengeance for a significant wrong done to you Defending a settlement from attacking monsters Commissioning great works of Art Taking a great personal risk to discover something Your piety score to Helios decreases if you diminish Helios’s influence in the world, contradict his ideals, or make him look weak and cowardly through acts such as these: Breaking an explicit promise or oath Violating any just law Putting others at risk through your own cowardice Rejection of a path forward because the potential for harm -Helios’s Devotee Piety 3+ Helios trait. As a devotee of Helios, you have proven yourself a worthy champion of the sun god. You can call on Helios’s favor and cast bless with this trait, requiring no material components. Helios’s blessing manifests as a nimbus around the affected creatures, causing them to shed dim light in a 5-foot radius until the spell ends. You can cast the spell in this way a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once) or with spell slots. You regain all expended free uses when you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.  -Helios’s Votary Piety 10+ Helios trait You can cast daylight with this trait once, or with spell slots. You regain your free use when you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell. In addition, you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.  -Helios’s Disciple Piety 25+ Helios trait You grow accustomed to the blazing radiance of the sun; you have advantage on saving throws against being blinded, and you have resistance to fire damage.  -Champion of the Sun Piety 50+ Helios trait You can increase your Strength or Wisdom score by 2 and also increase your maximum for that score by 2.

title: Maahes of Shield and Blade
content: I don't think anyone has used Maahes original name in an age. I think I'll try to find some old Thracian relics where it's spelled out explicitly, and then found an organization of scholars and meditative monks to "pry open their secrets". That will really rile him up, and then we'll have some opportunities for profit. -Reyvan Maahes is a Major God Regional Names: Polemos (Thracia), Hargon (Sendria), The Fury (The Dal, Subdurni) Domains: Forge, Life, Light, Order, War. Regional Worship: The Battlesworn (Thracia), The Brotherhood of Mithril Skin (The Shining Vale) by way of Kor, The Goliath of The Dal and the Hobgoblins and Goliath both of The Wastes of Subdurni Nature: Maahes lives for the glory of battle, preferring honorable combat and challenge over brutality and deceptive tactics. He does not explicitly champion the causes of good, civilization, or justice, but because those causes more often lead to glory than chaos, he has become intertwined with them. Maahes reaches out to the mortals under his influence in visions, dreams, and rituals. A call to greater fury, a rejection of the fear of death, a compulsion to take up arms and do what they know must be done, in the full light of everyone involved; these are some of the ways mortals hear the call of The War-Horn of Maahes. In particular, he tries his best to court anyone he thinks is susceptible to the path of Zatar. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Kor, the Tireless, Aspect of Endurance Relations to other Deities: Maahes bears a particular resentment to the relatively new god Zatar the Ravaging Storm. Although Maahes would have struck Cimmeria down, had he had the chance, the duplicitous way it was done and the harm it has caused The Eastern Continent aggrieves him. Origins: Maahes originated in what is now Thracia during the invasion of Apophis, which triggered the Sipparan War. Defeating Apophis after his Ascent to Godhood, Maahes's sword-stroke left the land scarred- they call it the Tiger's Stripe now, with a wide swath of desecrated land beyond it. I remember that Maahes was much more directly involved in the mortal world back then, and shaped what has become the Thracian civilization directly; I believe he wanted to prepare them to fight the undead menace in the Haunted Land. But, as many of the early gods discovered, interfering simply caused problems; the belligerence he gave the Thracians has caused much civil war and strife, leaving the Haunted Land still unconquered, and his physical presence shattered their land into the archipelago we find today. Activity: Maahes like war, there's no subtlety and nothing mor to it. He encourages it, but with all the normal niceties of a formal theatre of combat. Simple, but effective, given how easy mortals are to influence to violence. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Training, war, perhaps some light skirmishing before lunch, then advocating for military funding before having another nice war before the end of day. No attacks after dark, because that is cowardly; unless against a vastly superior foe, of course, in which case it's "tactically advantageous". There's nothing like a creed filled with self-serving rationale. Piety Benefits: You increase your piety score to Maahes when you expand the god’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these: Achieving a great victory Overcoming long odds honorably Defeating a skilled foe in single combat, particularly a follower of Zatar. Winning a great feat of strength or skill Your piety score to Maahes decreases if you diminish Maahes’s influence in the world, contradict his ideals, or make him look weak and cowardly through acts such as these: Showing cowardice in battle Besting an honorable foe through deceit Harming innocents or non-combatants -Maahes’s Devotee Piety 3+ Maahes trait. As a devotee of Maahes, you have earned his favor through victories won in his name. You can cast compelled duel with this trait a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), or with spell slots. You regain all expended free uses when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell. -Maahes’s Votary Piety 10+ Maahes trait. You can cast crusader’s mantle with this trait or with spell slots. You regain your free use when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell. In addition, you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened. -Maahes’s Disciple Piety 25+ Maahes trait. As a bonus action, you can call on Maahes’s blessing, gaining the following benefit for 1 minute or until you are incapacitated: creatures within 30 feet of you can’t gain advantage on attack rolls against you. Once you use this trait, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest. -Champion of Victory Piety 50+ Maahes trait. You can increase your Strength or Charisma score by 2 and also increase your maximum for that score by 2.

title: Rrakash of Tears and Torment
content: Now this one is exciting. A wizard, tortured by the fruits of his own knowledge, into becoming so much more. Why in all the planes above and below didn't I think of that? -Reyvan Rraskash is a Major God Regional Names: None Domains: Arcana, Death, Knowledge, Tempest. Regional Worship: None Nature: Rrakash, the God of Pain, appears as a creature of yellow energy, whose form contains and consumes a caged humanoid form semi-visible within. Rrakash is a new god, and has taken to appearing in a seemingly random fashion. His victims are not always killed, but those that survive generally wish they hadn't. Divinations describe the attacks as "feedings". Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: None Relations to other Deities: Emnity with Chorai due to their action in mortal life/unlife/it's actually very unclear. Origins: Born in anticipation and parellel to the ascent of Chorai, Rrakash, then Esiphin Steelbender, was the experimental partner the necromancer Soza Hakade, who later would become Chorai. Their experiments resulted in marked improvements in the field of arcane cloning. Meeting academic rejection instead of the praise she'd anticipated, Soza turned her studies further to the predictable (and honestly quite trite) goal of achieving lichdom. Esiphin balked at this and left the study of magic entirely. But each had created hundreds of clones. Esiphin, neglecting this, perhaps pretending it didn't happen- who can say- eventually dies of natural causes. And then, for centuries, was reborn again and again every six months. Sometimes he drowned in his cloning pod. Sometimes he was killed by their laboratory's defenses. Sometimes by the throng of undead (former clones) who had naturally remained nearby in their hidden facility. But he remembered it all, and went quite mad. The necromancy twisted over the years, he is empowered by this pain and seeks to experience as much as possible; himself or vicariously through others. Known only through divination and by a few who have been unfortunate enough to meet him, Rrakash's Ascent to Godhood seems to have occurred in Limbo, on the border to the Astral Plane. Given the timeless nature of those places, the consequences are not yet known; but with Limbo, anything is possible. No Minor Gods or Aspects have emerged relating to Rrakash. Activity: Feedings, as described. Desired Behaviour in Followers: N/A Piety Benefits: Unknown at this time.

title: Thalassus of the Oceans
content: The old soggy King of the Depths knows a thing or two about patience. I wonder what he and the Earth Mother discussed when they were the only True Gods in this realm. He'd never tell, and she doesn't talk. -Reyvan Thalassus is a Major God Regional Names: Argos (Thracia), The Drowning One, The Depths which Remember, Wise Uncle Domains: Knowledge,Nature,Tempest Regional Worship: Travellers (Thracia), Sounders (Redhill, The Shining Vale) Nature: The God of the Sea, Thalassus is the deep darkness, the constant provider and also the fickle destroyer. A source of constant slow change and renewal, Thalassus is a patron of underwater animals and peoples, druids, fishermen, explorers, sailors, and traders. Conservative minded sages often invoke the name of Thalassus, or his regional name, when urging caution in experiments. Thalassus guides his followers safely to distant shores, providing the proper offerings are made, and confounds the ships of the unfaithful and disrespectful. Thalassus is constant and less active in the affairs of the world, as his position can not be contested even by the other Gods. He is older than most of the Gods, and rich with the ancient knowledge he has collected, and sees efforts from the other gods to impose order as folly. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Anuket, the Flowing Water, Aspect of Collecting Aethenius, the Scholar, Aspect of Knowledge Relations to other Deities: Thalassus and Volkanus are in active opposition, and their followers will sometimes become violent. Origins: The original mortal ruler, King Apsu-Toli ruling the Bassarian culture, Thalassus's Ascent to Godhood caused the Sundering of the Lands, an event which split the continents apart- filling the voids left behind with the majesty of his waters. This is the first time that the ascent of a Major God created catastrophe, but this has been the most common result since. Desired Behavior in Followers: You increase your piety score to Thalassus when you expand the god’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these: Supporting those who would reform or overturn institutions Preventing cataclysmic change Offering a treasure to the sea Defending or maintaining a temple to Thalassus Your piety score to Thalassus decreases if you diminish Thalassus’s influence in the world, contradict his ideal of gradual change, or attempt to impose artificial order through acts such as these: Trying to keep a secret from Thalassus Using magic to calm the sea’s fury Upholding an institution not devoted to Thalassus Bowing to the desires or demands of another god Piety Benefits: -Thalassus’s Devotee Piety 3+ Thalassus trait. As a devotee of Thalassus, you have proven yourself a worthy representative of the god of the sea. You learn fog cloud with this trait. Fog created in this way smells strongly of the sea. You can cast the spell a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), or with a spell slot. You regain all expended free uses when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for this spell.  -Thalassus’s Votary Piety 10+ Thalassus trait. You learn blink with this trait. You can cast the spell in this way once, or with a spell slot. You regain the free use when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for this spell.  -Thalassus’s Disciple Piety 25+ Thalassus trait. You are inspired by the tempestuous, uncontrollable nature of the sea; you have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or restrained.  -Champion of Victory Piety 50+ Thalassus trait. You can increase your Dexterity or Intelligence score by 2 and also increase your maximum for that score by 2.

title: Atropos
content: I once saw an old man swear and spew hysterics at the name of Atropos, and then laugh even more when he wasn't struck down. He died 4 years later. I like to think she found his tiny outrage amusing, but grew resentful of it over time. -Reyvan Atropos is a Major God Regional Names: The Crone (Common), The Old Bag (derogatory, but never punished), She who severs all threads. Domains: Arcana, Death, Grave, Knowledge Regional Worship: As with the other fates, specific worship of Atropos is rare; but there are several religious orders in Thracia and beyond that strive to mercilessly eradicate the undead and those who create them; when not busy, they often serve as judges and executioners. Unusually for immortals, some elves of cruel persuasion find themselves in this calling. Nature: Atropos is the heartless being that ends all mortal life, the final arbiter of your story and the one who knows all that came before. The Old Crone, she is a haunting, terrifying and comforting figure all in one. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Sister to Clotho and Lachesis Relations to other Deities: It is assumed that Atropos will strongly oppose Chorai, once they become active. Origins: I think it would be disrespectful to discuss the beginning of the One Who Ends, and so I shall defer to the imagination of the reader. Activity: Almost everything ends. She must be busy, or have a lot of help. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Be relentless in ending that which needs to end, even more so for something that has come back from after its time. Shun the resurrected, and never participate in such things yourself. However, mourn, celebrate, and respect the properly dead. Commune with their spirits if you wish, but be civil; they have obeyed the order of things. Remember the lessons of your life. Piety Benefits: You increase your piety score to Atropos when you expand the goddess’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these: Preventing a person from living beyond their time Destroying an Undead creature in Atropos’ name Using past history to predict an important outcome. Your piety score to Atropos decreases if you diminish Atropos or the Fates’ influence in the world, contradict their ideals, or make her look weak or unimportant through acts like these: Creating or controlling an undead creature, or allowing one to exist. Resurrecting a creature, or allowing a resurrection to happen. Failing to learn from past examples. Atropos’s Devotee Piety 3+ Atropos trait. As a devotee of Atropos, you have earned her favor through wise acts in her name. You can cast inflict wounds with this trait once per long rest and with spell slots. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell. Atropos’s Votary Piety 10+ Atropos trait. You can cast ray of enfeeblement You can cast inflict wounds with this trait once per long rest and with spell slots. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell. In addition, you are immune to effects which would reduce your maximum hit points or change your age. Atropos’s Disciple Piety 25+ Atropos trait. As a bonus action, you can call on Atropos’s blessing and cast blight. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell. Once you use this trait, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest. Paragon of Experience Piety 50+ Atropos trait. You can increase your Constitution or Wisdom score by 2 and also increase your maximum for that score by 2.

title: Clotho
content: Clotho is the most banal, ineffectual, weak being I know of. That is to say, she is unusually kind and gracious. -Reyvan Clotho is a Major God Regional Names: The Spinner (common), The Maiden, (common) Domains: Life, Peace Regional Worship: Venerated nearly everywhere by people, particularly midwives and farmers with breeding flocks. Is often, but not always, depicted as the lover of Estes, the Eastern Wind in fertility cults. Nature: Clotho creates new lives and is very invested in nurturing them. She knows that they will end, but mourns each and every one. She is a tranquil being of harmony, peace and creation. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Lady Saoirse, the Midwife, Aspect of Childbirth Relations to other Deities: Sister to Lachesis and Atropos. Presumably violently angry at Chorai and Zatar, but you wouldn't know it as she's so damned wholesome. Origins: Did she originate, or is she constantly being born? It would fit, surely. Otherwise, no clue. Activity: Well.....babies. I think that should suffice as proof. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Encourage life and love, grow communities and collective support of mothers and babies. Piety Benefits: You increase your piety score to Clotho when you expand the god’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these: Helping a new life enter the world in a difficult situation Helping a peaceful community gain the resources they need to grow, in Clotho’s name Safeguarding the lineage of an important person. Your piety score to Clotho decreases if you diminish Clotho or the Fates’ influence in the world, contradict their ideals, or make her look weak or unimportant through acts like these: Killing, or allowing the unnecessary death of, children, the elderly and particularly mothers. Encouraging war for the sake of war Causing or encouraging acts which will harm the general populace, like a coup or excess taxation. -Clotho’s Devotee Piety 3+ Clotho trait. As a devotee of Clotho, you have earned her favor through generous acts in her name. You can cast cure wounds with this trait once per long rest, or with spell slots. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell. -Clotho’s Votary Piety 10+ Clotho trait. You can cast create food and water with this trait once per long rest with this trait, or with spell slots. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell. In addition, those who eat the food you create with this spell or goodberry are immune to the poisoned and diseased conditions until they finish a long rest. -Clotho’s Disciple Piety 25+ Clotho trait. As a bonus action, you can call on Clotho’s blessing and cast aura of life. Once you use this trait, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest. -Embodiment of Giving Piety 50+ Clotho trait. You can increase your Wisdom or Charisma score by 2 and also increase your maximum for that score by 2.

title: Lachesis
content: I am writing this commentary because it was foretold. Or pre-ordained. Perhaps both. -Reyvan Lachesis is a Major God Regional Names: The Grand Weaver (common, not to be confused with her spider-girl lackey), The Mother (not uncommon, but not usually used. Domains: Arcana, Knowledge, Trickery Regional Worship: Offerings are commonly made everywhere, but more to ward away her attention than to gain her benefit. Nature: Lachesis is a master designer, patron of those who make intricate plans, and those who seek foreknowledge. As such, her adherents include more than just clerics, but also spies, inventors, politicians and criminals. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Fantis, the Weaver of Beads Relations to other Deities: Sister to Clotho and Atropos. Origins: Well, where does one begin? At the end, I think. Here is the currently known truth, but as you'll see- it may not will have always be untrue for long. The Tale of Hazidkal Activity: How would you even begin to tell? And if you did, is it because she made you do it? Desired Behaviour in Followers: Generally serve as diviners. A conduit to the mortal realm is a useful thing for the Grand Weaver to have Piety Benefits: You increase your piety score to Lachesis when you expand the goddess’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these: - Defeating a creature that has stepped out of its place, particularly in a way that punishes them for all to see  - Repairing a significant wound dealt to destiny by the gods’ ambitions  - Bringing a reluctant person to accept the destiny given to them Your piety score to Lachesis decreases if you diminish Lachesis or the Fates’ influence in the world, contradict their ideals, or make her look weak or unimportant through acts like these:  - Undoing a deserved punishment or curse suffered by another creature  - Changing the fate of a creature, even if following a God’s command  - Assisting a creature in exploiting destiny, or exploiting it yourself    -Lachesis’s Devotee  Piety 3+ Lachesis trait.  As a devotee of Lachesis, you have earned her favor through clever acts in her name. You can cast comprehend languages with this trait a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) or using spell slots. You regain all free uses when you complete a long rest, and Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for this spell.    -Lachesis’s Votary Piety 10+ Lachesis trait. You can cast detect thoughts with this trait or with spell slots. You regain your free use when you complete a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for this spell.  In addition, you are immune to effects which would detect your mind, your creature type or alignment, or read your thoughts.    -Lachesis’s Disciple Piety 25+ Lachesis trait. As a bonus action, you can call on Lachesis’s blessing and cast divination. Once you use this trait, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.    -Paragon of Knowledge Piety 50+ Lachesis trait. You can increase your Intelligence or Wisdom score by 2 and also increase your maximum for that score by 2. Remove these ads. Join the Worldbuilders Guild

title: The Fates
content: The Grey Sisters are an oddity in terms of philosophy, epistemology and morality. But they're decent enough to bring their own contrasting flavours, so one shouldn't complain. -Reyvan The Fates are Major Gods Regional Names: Most often simply referred to as "The Fates" collectively, there are some references that name them as "The Grey Sisters", "Maiden-Mother-Crone", or "The Moirai". I believe I even came across a Bassarian reference calling them "Those stupid bitch gods", but I couldn't find a second piece of writing from that author, so they may simply have been in a bad mood. Domains: Each of the Fates has their own Domain list. Regional Worship: The Fates are more widely recognized than most other Ascended Gods, but are not strongly worshipped. It is common to find a temple or a shrine to the untended. In the case of Thracia, and in isolated places elsewhere, one may find at a temple a collection of The Fatebound; these priestesses (for they are always women) will always contain, support, and replace, a triad of seers. Nature: The Fates, deemed to be in control of the destinies of all living things and possibly of the Gods themselves, are always depicted as three sisters; Clotho , Lachesis and Atropos . Clotho spins the strands of Fate, Lachesis weaves its tapestry, and Atropos cuts the threads at their end; as such, they represent the beginning and end of life and everything in between. The Fates, like The Earth Mother, are omnipresent in the activity of mortals. Whether this counts as activity or as passive observation depends largely on your opinion on the existence of free will. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: See the individual Fates Relations to other Deities: The Fates seem to have a strained relationship with themselves sometimes- not the individual sisters, mind you, but each fate with themself. Lachesis particularly suffers from this, but the tribulations happen so far above the mortal (and indeed, immortal) point of view as to be indecipherable. Origins: See the individual Fates Activity: As described above, do the Fates meddle? Are they active? Better minds than mine have gone insane trying to puzzle that out. They are certainly knowledgeable, and seem to know what they are doing....and seem to, in fact, be doing things. Desired Behaviour in Followers: See the individual Fates Piety Benefits: Clerics of the Fates align with one of the three aspects, and often serve as Diviners to nobility. Unlike with clerics of other deities, it is very natural for an adherent to progress through worship of each Fate in order through their life. Any Piety gained when the time comes is kept, though the exiting rewards are lost when the new ones are gained.

title: Volkanus the Maker and Reshaper of all Things
content: Where Helios strives for improvement, Volkanus strives for change and novelty. I find this much more relatable, if less easy to parse. If Helios would teach mortals to put fire behind glass and have a lantern, for example, Volkanus is just as likely to teach them to put fire into bread, so they could cut open a loaf and eat toast in the morning. He leaves people in such confused danger, they're easy pickings. But above all else, if you can steal from him- he does make such incredible toys... -Reyvan Volkanus is a Major God Regional Names: Moradin (Dwarven Cultural), St. Isolde (The Shining Vale VR 120-PI), Domains: Forge, Knowledge, War Regional Worship: Among human cultures, worship of Volkanus is generally either as a Fire Cult (Thracia), individual eccentrics, or a proper human-lead church as in The Shining Vale. Highly variable, they could be anywhere- but the signs of madness that tend to follow them aren't difficult to spot. Among dwarves, he is venerated as their father; they work tirelessly to create makings worthy of him. Gnomes aren't uncommon in either the human or dwarven worship scenarios, as their natural curiosity lends well to the practice. In both cases, though, they seem to succumb to the more manic expressions of interest and faith. My personal favourite is in the Vale, because they don't seem to notice or care that they are venerating a Gnomish Saint that isn't dead and who actively protested the use of her name and story, in person, before giving up and straight-up leaving time. Priceless. -Reyvan Nature: Volkanus is the god of the forge, the restless earth, and fire. He rules the raw creative force that infuses sapient minds. Volkanus is also the god of artisans, obsession, mania, and the cycle of creation and destruction. As a forge radiates heat in the area around it, Volkanus’s influence provides inspiration to mortals. He makes exquisitely crafted objects almost constantly, sometimes absentmindedly working while he holds conversations with the other gods, only to destroy the finished product and begin again. Impulsive and mercurial, Volkanus is prone to bouts of either joyous productivity or frustrated anger. He often feels constrained by the limits of imagination, yearning to realize ideas that seem just out of reach. Volkanus’s preferred form is that of a muscular man whose coal-hued skin is mostly covered in mutable organic bronze. He might also appear in the form of a fiery phoenix or a bull made of cooling lava, and for that reason, both of those creatures are associated with him. When angered, he might appear as an enormous mass of lava, a blazing fire, or a volcanic eruption. Mortals who see Volkanus in one of those forms seldom live to tell about it. Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Dun, the Smelter, Aspect of Labour Lady Racca, the Planner, Aspect of Construction Relations to other Deities: Volkanus, nearly alone among the gods, is an advocate for Zatar the Ravaging Storm. I assume it's because the destruction leaves so many more pieces for Volkanus to work with. Volkanus and Thalassus are in active opposition, and their followers will sometimes become violent. Origins: Volkanus arose in Thracia during the height of Maahes' influence, as the land was being shattered and splintered by his presence. In a relatively peaceful Ascent to Godhood, the stories say that the large volcano, named for the God, of course, that grew between the warring cities of Agaria and Brekaris was the beginning of the end for Maahes's time on this realm; when he saw how much damage he was causing to such an inferior people, his shame demanded his retreat. Volkanus rose up, and The Shield of Thracia, the mountain range now present, sealed almost a year of peace. Land was filled in and several nearby islands were rejoined to the mainland, which likely began the feud with Thalassus, but Volkanus did not remain on this realm in person. He uses his Minor Gods and Aspects as proxies for his will, often sending them to meet face-to-face with his most devoted followers; the dwarves. Activity: It is known that Volkanus created the dwarves shortly after he ascended, and they venerate him as their father. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits: Earning and Losing Piety  You increase your piety score to Volkanus when you expand the god’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these:  - Fighting against those who would rule over others  - Taking decisive action on impulse  - Destroying something that has outlived its usefulness  - Creating something wondrous in Volkanus’s name Your piety score to Volkanus decreases if you diminish Volkanus’s influence in the world, work against freedom or self-expression, or allow tyranny to take hold through acts such as these:  - Following an unjust law despite your misgivings  - Creating something shoddy or flawed  - Backing down from a fight or a contest -Volkanus’s Devotee Piety 3+ Volkanus trait. As a devotee of Volkanus, you have drawn the notice of the forge god with your skill. You can cast shield of faith with this trait, requiring no material components, a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) and with spell slots. You regain all expended free uses when you finish a long rest.  In addition, you know the mending cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.    -Volkanus’s Votary Piety 10+ Volkanus trait. You can cast heat metal with this trait, requiring no material components. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest, or if you use a spell slot. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for this spell. In addition, you have advantage on saving throws against being knocked prone.    -Volkanus’s Disciple Piety 25+ Volkanus trait. While you are standing and an effect would push you 1 foot or more, you can use your reaction to not be pushed, or to be pushed up to 50 feet, at your preference. Additionally, you can take 10 minutes to create any mundane item, providing you sacrifice an equivalent value of other items or coin.   -Champion of Mountains Piety 50+ Volkanus trait. You can increase your Strength or Intelligence score by 2 and also increase your maximum for that score by 2.

title: Zatar the Ravaging Storm
content: The newest of the Ascended Gods, until recently, and certainly she's been a busy, busy god! It's a breath of fresh-corpse-sulfur-and-ash tinted air! I hope the new ones take her lead. -Reyvan* Zatar is a Major God Regional Names: Zatar's established faith has not yet had time to spread so much that the name is lost. Domains: Death, Tempest, War Regional Worship: Established worship is, as of yet, limited to tribal groups and fanatic cults. This is expected to change over the coming centuries. Nature: Zatar is the cruel and vicious Goddess of carnage, destruction and storms. Tearing her way through the world with no goals beyond death and disarray, she is a force that most of the other Gods defend against in concert, turning her away to wreak havoc elsewhere. Her adherents, the Bloodbound, are not welcome in polite society, and generally do not hide themselves there; they are the wild barbarian, the vengeful druid, the destructive spellcaster, and others who simply wish to kill, kill, kill for no reason other than their own drives. Rumour says she is also the source of lycanthropy, though this remains unconfirmed and would fly in the face of known history. Nice embellishment, though. - Reyvan Associated Minor Deities/Aspect Deities: Relations to other Deities: Zatar has relentless aggression towards Maahes, which is likely competition for the same pool of adherents. Despite favorable motions from Volkanus towards Zatar and on her benefit, there has been no notable accommodations in the other direction. I assume, but have not yet observed, that when Rrakash becomes more well known, he and Zatar will either join forces, given their shared goals and prior hatred of necromancers, or attempt to tear each other apart. It's quite possible that both will happen, which will leave spoils for those like me. -Reyvan Origins: Zatar's Ascent to Godhood is the most clearly documented, as the more recent ascensions were only witnessed by a few adventurers, who were more or less causal in the path they took. In life, Isthariel the Channeler was likely the fifth or sixth most powerful wizard on the mortal plane, hindered more by her lack of subtlety than her actual prowess. See her entry for more detail, but the end result was the Fall of Cimmeria and the end of Cimmerian Empire, along with a few dead and/or tortured Minor Gods. It is my personal belief, based on a few decades of study, that the Fall of Cimmeria, essentially a mithril hammer striking the ground with immense force, is what has disrupted the carefully laid, and centuries-long neglected, lines of power of The Luxan Empire in what is now called The Shining Vale, Subdurni, Lorica, The High Depths and The Fernish Jungle. Please see the entry on Callamus the Righteous and his conquest of the Shining Vale for more details. - Reyvan Activity: Zatar has been very active since her ascension. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Zatar wishes for chaotic destruction, and nothing less. Her followers tend toward raiding, razing, ruining everything in their local environment. Among their own people, contests are often brutal and lethal. Piety Benefits: You increase your piety score to Zatar when you expand the goddess’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these:  - Defeating a champion of Maahes  - Taking vengeance against a powerful foe  - Burning a polis or a settlement to the ground  - Desecrating a temple to Maahes as an offering to Zatar    Your piety score to Zatar decreases if you diminish Zatar’s influence in the world, contradict her ideals, or make her look weak or ineffectual through acts such as these:  - Failing to carry out a sworn vendetta  - Publicly displaying weakness or compassion  - Rejecting a duel or a challenge out of fear -Zatar’s Devotee Piety 3+ Zatar trait. As a devotee of Zatar, you have shown yourself to be a brutal combatant. You can call on Zatar’s favor and cast wrathful smite with this trait. Zatar’s blessing manifests as a blue glow around your weapon, causing it to shed dim light in a 5-foot radius until the spell ends. You can cast the spell in this way a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once), or with spell slots. You regain all expended free uses when you finish a long rest. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell. -Zatar’s Votary Piety 10+ Zatar trait. You can cast blinding smite with this trait once per long rest, or with spell slots. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell. -Zatar’s Disciple Piety 25+ Zatar trait. Countless battles and vicious visions have given you focus. You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened, or immunity if you already had advantage. -Champion of Slaughter Piety 50+ Zatar trait. You can increase your Strength or Constitution score by 2 and also increase your maximum for that score by 2.

title: Major Gods
content: Astere of the Night Sky Chorai the Stillborn Dead Helios the Radiant Truth Maahes of Shield and Blade Rrakash of Tears and Torment Thalassus of the Oceans The Earth Mother Irithiel The Fates Volkanus the Maker and Reshaper of all Things Zatar the Ravaging Storm

title: Aethenius, the Scholar
content: Ah, my good and very sheltered friend Aethenius. Where Paratiritis sees everything and understands little, Aethenius understands a great deal- but sees little. He can be easily tricked, with dire consequences for failure. -Reyvan Aethenius is a Minor God Regional Names: Ptolemus (Thracia), He Who Brings the World to Him. Domains: Arcana, Knowledge, Order. Regional Worship: Statues of Aethenius as a wise owl, with or without horns, are often found at archives, libraries and other places of collected knowledge, but it has been so long since an active worship of him existed that it's likely that the architect of those places simply see them as appropriate dressing, ignorant of the religious meaning. Nature: Aethenius is very bright, very eager, and incredibly harsh with those who lie or hold back knowledge. He collects knowledge on every subject, His normal form, an owl with stag horns, has not been seen in the world for some ages. Associated Major Deity: Thalassus of the Oceans Relations to other Deities: Origins: When the Bassarian culture was at its height, and the King Apsu-Toli was most venerated by his people, some very strange things began to happen. After Karda Basmir, who became Anuket, failed to stop the moving water and did not return, King Apsu-Toli set his highest sage to discover the reason for the change and develop a plan to end the issue. This sage, Apkallu who Reads, buried himself in the words of the most eminent philosophers, priests and magicians of the land, sending a thousand scribes out of the city of Bassaria to the distant corners of the kingdom. The more he learned, and the more desperate he became to give King Apsu-Toli a solution, the more he isolated himself. His form changed as he had been chosen as the second mortal to ascend to become a Minor God. Activity: Aethenius spends all of his time at his Library, The Aethenium, the location of which is a closely guarded secret. There he gathers, sort and hoards knowledge. Yes, I do know where the Aethenium is. No, I'm not going to tell you. Aethenius is a kindred spirit, a good friend, and more importantly an irreplaceable source of knowledge for me. -Reyvan Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Anuket, the Flowing Water
content: Anuket is a product of her Master- constant (most of the time), direct (most of the time), and helpful (most of the time). It's the other times where you need to watch your back. -Reyvan Anuket is a Minor God Regional Names: Achelus (Thracia), She Who Feeds the Sea Domains: Nature, Tempest Regional Worship: Not commonly worshiped, but with isolated shrines in river-bound fishing villages or small towns prone to flooding. Nature: Anuket, ironically given her role, is best described as "Still waters run deep". She will be calm and steady until the moment she flips; and either becomes very friendly or very hostile. Her role is to bring people, material and ideas to her Master, Thalassus. This very functional role keeps her busy most of the time. Associated Major Deity: Thalassus of the Oceans Relations to other Deities: Origins: When the Bassarian culture was at its height, and the King Apsu-Toli was most venerated by his people, some very strange things began to happen. The first of these was the movement of the waters. Snow-capped mountains began to shed their white covering, and ice began to melt for the first time, and the water began to collect in lowlands. As this started to flood and overwhelm the residents of his country, King Apsu-Toli instructed his third minister, Karda Basmir, to fix the situation. He had no way of knowing this, but he had just decided on the ascent of the first mortal to become a Minor God. After heading out, when Karda experienced the world's first river, she began to bend its course, trying to avoid the houses and farms that she could. This proved impossible at the time, and many drowned. Activity: Anuket brings everything she can to the realm of her master, which keeps her busy most of the time. To aid her, she invites natural spirits to occupy each river and stream, which has resulted in the Naiad population we see today. Anuket herself works mostly as an administrator, echoing her former mortal life, hearing the petitions of her Naiads as mortals affect the rivers. In recent times, Anuket suffered greatly in the Fall of Cimmeria, being grievously wounded by the impact- collateral damage rather than an intentional target. Thalassus looks to restore her, so she now recuperates in the bay that resulted by the fall, unknowingly entrapping Jaraxx, the Rot. Her absence since then has resulted in more river-work industry among the mortals, and a decided increase in hostility in the Naiad. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Frouros, the Watchman
content: The best I can say about Frouros is that every joke needs a straightman. -Reyvan Frouros is a Minor God Regional Names: Colossus (Thracia), Vigilance, He Who Watches So We May Rest Domains: Forge, Order, Peace, War Regional Worship: Other than in Thracia, explicit worship of Frouros has gone out of fashion- with most large nations being more interested in warfare instead of defensive oversight, when it comes to battle. Occasionally a city state, castle, or other target will start to worship him though, so he is not entirely relegated to the background or to common sayings, like some gods have been. Nature: Frouros stands as a sentinel between the mortals of this world and the creatures that want to invade. He is stoic, often doesn't speak, and simply does what he came to do and then leaves. Frouros most often chooses to appears as an immense statue of gleaming silvery metal, standing between 12 feet and 200 feet tall depending on his needs. He usually does not hide his presence, but has been known to do so in order to foil covert activities. His non-metallic form is usually that of a goliath or very tall human man. Associated Major Deity: Astere of the Night Sky Relations to other Deities: Openly antagonistic with his brother, Trikar, the Shrike. Origins: Fouros and Trikar were brothers in mortal life, though they took very different paths- the Night Watchman and the Hidden Killer. Their adversarial contests and battles grew to be so epic over time that Astere was impressed. She offered to take them both into her fold- Trikar being gleefully accepting of the new power, and Frouros accepting it begrudgingly so that his brother could not escape him. To this day, their battle is ongoing both personally and via the mortal proxies of their old stations. Activity: Frouros is a very active God, physically moving from place to place to help build defenses, lay traps, keep order and above all else keep watch for extra-planar entities wishing harm on mortals- particularly his broker Trikar. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Paratiritis, the Seeing
content: An unstoppable voyeur who can breach all but the most powerful of protections, and see all? Why, oh why was I not so endowed. The only saving grace I see (yes, that joke was intentional) is that I at least have the capacity to care about the things I investigate. -Reyvan Paratiritis is a Minor God Regional Names: He Who Sees All and Understands Nothing, The Eye Domains: Arcana, Knowledge Regional Worship: None. Paratiritis is sometimes evoked by Oracles and Diviners, but has no established worship. Nature: Paratiritis is the God of Observers and those who look for danger in dark places; these could be vigilantes, night watchmen, hunters, robbers, assassins, thieves, or other hidden assailants. He manifests as an eye, typically 5 to 6 feet in diameter, surrounded in two rows of grey feathers which rotate around the eye in opposite directions. He flits here and there, always in motion, always looking.  Paratiritis is known to be able to see anything, anywhere, including penetrating all but the strongest of magical barriers. Associated Major Deity: Astere of the Night Sky Relations to other Deities: Paratiritis is a close companion of Fantis, as he helps her see which mortals deserve new burdens and how the already burdened are coping. He is also on good terms with The Legion of the Dead, having met her when she was still Evette Blackthorn. Origins: As with his Mistress, Paratiritis' origins are a mystery. When she appeared, so did her watcher. He will happily discuss almost any topic with any non-hostile entity, but the extent of his memory is limited, and whether he actually understands most things is a questionable proposition. Activity: Paratiritis spends most of his time in the Great Temple in the Sky, where the Obelisk of Divination can be found, alongside Fantis. He has a good view from there, which doesn't matter in the sligtest to him of course, but it must be peaceful. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Trikar, the Shrike
content: I like Trikar personally; he is very driven and direct, despite his calling. Moreover, the thought that the secretive killing of mortals is an important enough impulse that a God was required is very good news for an entity like me. -Reyvan Trikar is a Minor God Regional Names: Janus (Thracia), He Who Kills With No Warning Domains: Trickery, War Regional Worship: Trikar tends to be venerated by very few civilized folk, as any church or cult to him is very quickly stamped out by the more civilized folk. Individual followers exist though, and are often found at the heart of a thieves' guild or similar organization enabling and blessing their activities. How they come to know of and worship Trikar is unknown and likely down to the individual. Nature: Trikar's outlook on mortals is fairly simple: they are prey to be taken down. Unlike Chiriki who epitomizes the hunting of beasts for food or glory, Trikar's motivation is simply the act itself. Trikar stokes the hearts of evil mortals, or those with the capacity for evil, drawing them closer to his mindset. Associated Major Deity: Astere of the Night Sky Relations to other Deities: Trikar is in an openly antagonistic relationship with his brother, Frouros, the Watchman. Origins: Trikar and Frouros were brothers in mortal life, though they took very different paths- the Hidden Killer and the Night Watchman. Their adversarial contests and battles grew to be so epic over time that Astere was impressed. She offered to take them both into her fold- Trikar being gleefully accepting of the new power, and Frouros accepting it begrudgingly so that his brother could not escape him. To this day, their battle is ongoing both personally and via the mortal proxies of their old stations. Activity: Trikar operates very much as devils do, offering power for services. Those whom he courts may not understand that those services just bring them further under his sway. When he is described, it is, unhelpfully, as "a person in the room that could not be seen". Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Pan, The Soulgatherer
content: As one of the newest minor gods, you would assume that Pan wouldn't know how to act and would be going through some process of discovery. You'd be wrong, but only because you weren't aware that he is a Cat. -Reyvan Pan is a Minor God Regional Names: He Who Crosses the Bridge Domains: Grave Regional Worship: None Nature: Taking the form of a Ghostly grey cat, Pan wanders here and there guiding dead souls to the afterlife. He is seemingly ambivalent to his role as a psychopomp. Associated Major Deity: Chorai the Stillborn Dead Relations to other Deities: Origins: Pan arose in anticipation of the ascent of Chorai. Activity: Pan generally travels with a chosen mortal, extending their life and providing them with power to be able to survive the journey. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: The Legion of the Dead
content: A new face, though one I think I'll see wandering the world for a very long time. -Reyvan The Legion of the Dead is a Minor God Regional Names: The Army Myriad, Soza Hakade (mistaken), She Who is Unending Domains: Arcana, Death Regional Worship: None Nature: The Legion walks the world alone; though the name makes it sound like there are many, there is only one at a time (barring exceptional circumstances). Associated Major Deity: Chorai the Stillborn Dead Relations to other Deities: Acquainted with Fantis, the Weaver of Beads and Paratiritis, the Seeing, and on reasonable terms with both. Origins: Born in anticipation of the ascent of Chorai, the legion is the result of the mortal efforts of the necromancer Soza Hakade, who later would become Chorai. Her experiments with her partner Esiphin Steelbender (who later became Rrakash) resulted in marked improvements in the field of arcane cloning. Meeting academic rejection instead of the praise she'd anticipated, Soza turned her studies further to the predictable (and honestly quite trite) goal of achieving lichdom. Esiphin balked at this and left the study of magic entirely. Soza, after finding the demilich remains of Quidun the Wise and Benificent, gained the secrets of permanent unlife and achieved lichdom- see the entry on Chorai for the rest of that story. But a sharp mind may ask- what happened to her clones once she abandoned mortality? It seems that her clones, after some time, gained sentience of their own. The cloning pods moved to follow the undead necromancer, though she could not see or feel them. The first few clones lived out lives in Sendria, with three out of four being persecuted for witchcraft in that backward place. Eventually the fifth clone emerged (naming itself Evette Blackthorn), and being in a strange situation with no memory, took it upon herself to escape. Fleeing to Port Rain, she eventually discovered her past, made allies (see The Seekers) and was part of the events that hampered the ascent of Soza, resulting in her becoming Chorai the Stillborn Dead rather than another, more active form of godhood. But the energies already present in the clones, as well as what the clone Evette drew from the Obelisks of Power that the Seekers encountered, finalised her position as a Minor God. Activity: The legion, still Evette Blackthorn at time of writing, wanders the land, studying and learning. She has found more of her future self in a few places, and has come to terms with the fact that there will never be an end to this. She has, at the least, solved the memory loss on transferal and had previously made contact with her past selves; but one wonders how much of her mortal soul will remain after a few centuries, a few millennia, a few ages. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Aesclepius, the Mender
content: Healing an injury used to be impossible for mortal folk, which meant I could do good business in the restoration or replacement of injured body parts. That is long past, now, but my memory is long and I am resentful. But I also would prefer not to be incinerated the the Sun God- who is likely the only one capable of doing so outside of the Elder Gods- not that he'd even note my small presence. -Reyvan Aesclepius is a Minor God Regional Names: St Lucia (Lausanne, The Shining Vale), He Who Restores Domains: Forge, Life Regional Worship: Temples to Aesclepius are rare, but a shrine or icon is present in many temples to other gods, as a show of respect to the original healer. Some workshops will do the same, for the mending of broken things is not so different. Nature: Aesclepius is often depicted as a patrician scholar, a learned man who understands the workings of the body and the soul, but in truth this depiction stems from the prejudices of mortal society more than of Aesclepius' true form. If a mortal ever saw the actual form of Aesclepius, a turquoise-scaled snake with human arms and legs, I'm not sure healing would be the thought in their mind. Aesclepius is literally the font of healing magic in the world, which means that any healing from any god, enchantment or potion, flows from him. He fuels this by inhabiting the very center of Helios' being, staying in the sun as it turns about the world. Associated Major Deity: Helios Relations to other Deities: Origins: In the aftermath of the Sundering of the Lands, in the reconstruction of Bassaria after Themis, Aesthenes, and Parvati has come into being and done their work, Helios was satisfied that mortals would no longer actively destroy themselves; at least, in the short term. Then, as is his way, he saw a way to improve the situation even more: what if they could not only not kill, but what if they could repair? Thus was born Aesclepius, the very idea of Healing. Activity: The constant draw on Aesclepius for healing has progressed to the point that he can not leave his position inside Helios, but he does have agents and followers in the world with whom he regularly communes. He uses them to promote the advancement of medicines and healing magic, and to ensure that needs in this regards are being met. And allow me to assure you, agents of the God of Healing are nearly impossible to kill. My ratio for success is below 10 percent. -Reyvan Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Aesthenes, the Glorious
content: One can not help but look at Aesthenes, it's looking away that takes willpower. Many have died making the attempt, generally of dehydration or exposure to the elements. -Reyvan Aesthenes is a Minor God Regional Names: St. Agatha (The Shining Vale, Lausanne), She Who Inspires Domains: Life, Light, Peace, Trickery Regional Worship: It is not uncommon for a temple to Aesthenes to bless and sell clothing and beauty products which are enchanted to provide an illusory appearance fot the clothing and/or the wearer. Nature: Generally depicted as a partially nude, picturesque woman, mortal artists trying to portray her always complain that they haven't got it quite right; they are correct, but they may not have realized that success in this effort is not possible. Aesthenes is the Muse of Beauty, and works to make all things beautiful. Her standard approach is to create and improve, only advocating destruction in the worst case scenarios. She works on people, lands, buildings, creatures- nothing is beyond her gaze. Associated Major Deity: Helios Relations to other Deities: Origins: During the reconstruction of Bassaria after the Sundering of the Lands, the once-majestic civilization was in ruin. Cities had been shaken to rubble, and sometimes even split by the newly-formed oceans; and despite the new rule of justice under Themis, the people still lost hope. Helios saw their despair and resolved to give them reason for hope and cheer; and thus in an instant was born Aesthenes, the very idea of Beauty. Activity: Aesthenes is one of the only minor gods that does not generally appear in her physical form to mortals, instead opting to visit them in dreams, visions and in reverie. The few times she does visit physically is when she needs to command rather than influence, to enthrall, compel or seduce Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Parvati, the Accepting
content: There is nothing quite as evil as being told to accept and forgive what your enemy has done. Do not listen to these seductive lies, there are always ways to make them pay; and if you can not think of any, I am willing to make a deal to help you. -Reyvan Parvati is a Minor God Regional Names: St. Annabella (The Shining Vale VR 50-PI, Lausanne), She Who Embraces Domains: Life, Light, Peace Regional Worship: Worship of Parvati is generally in the building of community; therefore she is often worshiped alongside Lady Racca. Nature: Parvati, she of open arms, is the Muse of Love. This encompasses romantic love, but also familial and community love, with a particular emphasis on tolerance, acceptance and forgiveness. Parvati wants the best for all mortals, and believes that if everyone accepted each other as they are, forgives others and themselves, and simply loves, that all problem and strife would soon end. Because drought, flood and the actions of other gods, of course can be solved by wishful thinking. -Reyvan Associated Major Deity: Helios Relations to other Deities: Origins: When the initial issues in Bassaria, following the Sundering of the Lands, had been addressed by Themis and Aesthenes, Helios saw the people harbouring lingering resentments, jealousy over the newfound beautiful things they had made, and beginning to fracture into tribes. Wanting to revive the glory of Bassaria, Helios wished for the people to accept and forgive the past, so as to be able to make a common future: thus was born Parvati, the very idea of Love. Activity: Parvati visits the various people of the world in many guises, de-escalating conflict and building community wherever she goes. Along with Lady Racca, she will help communities build common ground, literally and metaphorically. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Themis, the Righteous
content: There is very little room for interpretation in the perspective of Themis. It is light, or dark; good, or evil; just or heretical. That could trip a foolish mortal up, if given advice that places them in the grey. -Reyvan Themis is a Minor God Regional Names: St. Domingo (Duchy Truscia PI), He Who Ajudicates and Acts Domains: Order, War Regional Worship: Usually a human woman, in Duchy Truscia of The Shining Vale, Themis (as St. Domingo) is instead a male tiefling. Nature: Depicted with a sword and the scales of justice, Themis, the Muse of Justice, is an unwavering influence towards total order. Associated Major Deity: Helios Relations to other Deities: Themis does not have good relations with many of the other gods, including the other aspects of Helios, as their more nuanced approaches and less dogmatic domains each invoke at least some level of chaos in the mortal realm. The only god she is known to approve of is Atropos, as death and the natural order of the life cycle fall neatly into her rigid worldview. Origins: In the chaos following the Sundering of the Lands in Bassaria, mortal folk were no longer ruled by their once-mighty King and were desperate to survive. Infighting escalated, and Helios, the only active Major God at the time, was moved by their plight. He sought for a way to bring them the truth that they needed to band together to survive, and thus in an instant was born Themis, the very idea of Justice. Activity: Themis works to offset what she sees as the corrupting influence of the other gods, either posing as a zealous judge, or putting herself in a position to influence such people.
